behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff (classifier -> API client serialization), not model quality. MOCK-MISS: a pure unit test of a helper could miss dropped fields during wiring. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none required. Adversarial critique: if request building is a single pure function and that exact object is sent unchanged, Unit could be enough. Top2 contrast: Integration wins because the bug can occur at serialization/client boundary; flip to Unit if behavior text guaranteed no boundary (e.g., 'single pure builder function output is the sent payload'). Rejection-gate: System/Agentic are disqualified by behavior text focusing on request content, not real network effects or model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier with a fake API transport and assert outbound payload contains every configured taxonomy label on each call, including through real request serialization code path.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is local mapping/validation logic on returned label. MOCK-MISS: no module interaction is required to reproduce invalid-label handling. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no. Adversarial critique: if unknown-label handling is split across parser and service layers, Integration may be needed. Top2 contrast: Unit wins because one function can be fed an out-of-taxonomy label and must map to 'unknown'; flip to Integration if behavior text required cross-module transformation before validation.
behaviors.test_description: Call the label-validation/normalization logic with taxonomy plus an invalid provider label and assert result is exactly 'unknown'.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic normalization idempotence/purity at one local boundary. MOCK-MISS: cross-module wiring is not required to expose nondeterministic normalization bugs. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Adversarial critique: hidden global state (locale/cache/config) could make this an integration issue. Top2 contrast: Unit wins since identical input -> identical output is a pure function property; flip to Integration if normalization depends on mutable shared collaborators.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke normalization repeatedly with the same raw response fixture and taxonomy and assert identical final label each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires repeated live AI-provider calls and checks consistency tied to provider outputs; stubs hide model/provider variability. LLM-DEP is direct, MULTI-STEP is not (single decision per call). Adversarial critique: if this only compared cached raw responses, Agentic would be too high. Top2 contrast: Agentic beats System because correctness depends on model/provider decision behavior, not just HTTP transport; flip to System if API were deterministic rule-based and non-ML.
behaviors.test_description: Run multiple real classification calls against the provider for identical input, record raw provider outputs and final labels, and assert label changes only when provider response content changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure can occur at boundary between token-budget logic and dispatch path (e.g., computed budget not enforced before client call). No real OS/network/model-output dependence required. Adversarial critique: if budget enforcement is entirely inside one pure function, Unit suffices. Top2 contrast: Integration wins because test must prove pre-dispatch gate is honored in wired flow; flip to Unit if behavior text constrained scope to pure token-policy function only. Rejection-gate: Agentic/System disqualified because this behavior is pre-dispatch enforcement, not live provider/model effects.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier with deterministic tokenizer/counting component and fake client; send over-budget input and assert configured truncate/reject policy is applied before any outbound API dispatch.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: To catch real failure, test must hit real AI provider accounting/tokenization; mocks can falsely pass by reporting idealized usage. This traverses a real AI API call, so LLM-DEP default applies; not multi-step. Adversarial critique: if objective were only local preflight estimator math, Integration would be enough. Top2 contrast: Agentic beats System because assertion depends on provider/model token accounting semantics, not merely HTTP success; flip to System if provider were non-ML deterministic metering endpoint independent of model behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real provider calls for representative document(s), read provider-reported token usage from responses, and assert measured usage does not exceed configured budget for each call.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is retry orchestration across classifier, backoff policy, and client adapter. Can be reproduced with in-process stub returning rate-limit signal sequence; no real OS/network/model-quality required. Adversarial critique: provider-specific headers/timing quirks might require System to catch. Top2 contrast: Integration wins because deterministic stubbed 429/retry-after sequence can validate wait+retry behavior; flip to System if behavior required validating real provider timing semantics under live network conditions. Rejection-gate: Agentic disqualified since correctness is retry control flow, not model output content.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API client that returns rate-limit response then success; assert module waits per policy, retries, and returns successful label instead of surfacing an error.
